findings.title: Rebase abort skipped when rebase succeeds but tests fail in worktree merge
findings.summary: In mergeWorktreeBlock, the condition checks both rebaseAndTestSuccess being false AND rebaseRes.status being non-zero before calling git rebase --abort. When rebase succeeds but tests fail, the rebase is never aborted, leaving the worktree branch in a rebased state.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/phases/implement.ts:486 - condition `!rebaseAndTestSuccess && rebaseRes.status !== 0` requires both to be true; rebase-succeeded + test-failed path never triggers abort
packages/remediate-code/src/phases/implement.ts:470-484 - rebase success path sets rebaseAndTestSuccess only if tests pass; test failure leaves it false without issuing an abort
packages/remediate-code/src/phases/implement.ts:498-501 - worktree removed with --force regardless, so the branch is cleaned up, but no explicit abort is issued for the rebase

findings.title: splitBlocksByContextBudget copies stale dependency IDs to sub-blocks without updating dependent blocks
findings.summary: When splitBlocksByContextBudget splits one block into sub-blocks, the original block_id is replaced by suffixed IDs, but other blocks whose dependencies array referenced the original block_id are not updated. The topological sort silently skips missing IDs, breaking dependency ordering.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/phases/plan.ts:513-515 - sub-block IDs are `${block.block_id}-01` etc.; original block_id no longer exists in the returned blocks array
packages/remediate-code/src/phases/plan.ts:518 - `dependencies: block.dependencies` copies the original block deps but other blocks still list the now-gone original block_id as a dependency

packages/remediate-code/src/phases/implement.ts:107-114 - visit() does `blocks.find(b => b.block_id === dep)`; if dep is the original block_id (now absent), block is undefined and the dependency edge is silently dropped

findings.title: enumerateTestFiles passes 'list' as a file pattern rather than using the vitest list subcommand
findings.summary: The vitest invocation passes 'list' as a trailing positional argument, treating it as a file-path filter rather than invoking the vitest list subcommand. The verbose reporter output also contains test names rather than file paths, so the returned strings are not usable as file paths in deriveBlocksFromTestGraph.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/phases/plan.ts:42-48 - args `['vitest', '--reporter=verbose', '--run', '--passWithNoTests', 'list']`; 'list' is a pattern, not the list subcommand
packages/remediate-code/src/phases/plan.ts:54-61 - output filtered for lines containing '.'; verbose reporter emits test names with status icons, not absolute file paths
packages/remediate-code/src/phases/plan.ts:62 - misidentified test names returned as file paths and fed into deriveBlocksFromTestGraph where they are matched against source file path segments

findings.title: decideNextStep drops sessionConfig in recursive state-transition calls
findings.summary: When decideNextStep recurses after state transitions (implementing to triage, triage to next, documenting to implementing), it omits sessionConfig from the recursive call options. Any in-memory sessionConfig override supplied by the caller is silently lost; the re-entry re-reads from disk.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/src/steps/nextStep.ts:1447-1452 - recursive call for implementing status omits sessionConfig field
packages/remediate-code/src/steps/nextStep.ts:1456-1463 - recursive call for triage status omits sessionConfig field
packages/remediate-code/src/steps/nextStep.ts:1469-1474 - recursive call when documenting with no implementable blocks also omits sessionConfig
packages/remediate-code/src/steps/nextStep.ts:972-977 - re-entry reads session config from disk; a caller-supplied in-memory value that differs from the on-disk config is silently ignored

findings.title: recordWaveOutcomeUnsafe does not penalize lower-concurrency buckets on failure
findings.summary: On a non-success outcome, recordWaveOutcomeUnsafe adds failure weight only to buckets at concurrency..concurrency+4, leaving lower-numbered buckets (1..concurrency-1) with only accumulated success weight. computeMaxSafeConcurrency can therefore return optimistic concurrency levels after a wave failure at a given concurrency.
evidence: packages/shared/src/quota/state.ts:185-193 - failure weight loop starts at `outcome.concurrency`, buckets 1 through concurrency-1 receive no failure weight
packages/shared/src/quota/state.ts:97-108 - computeMaxSafeConcurrency returns highest n where success_weight > failure_weight; buckets below failure level remain with unpenalized success weight
packages/shared/src/quota/state.ts:30-44 - decay reduces both weights over time but does not immediately correct the over-optimistic lower buckets after a failure
